text: Model-agnostic prompt is a leaky abstraction — skills are prompt text + runtime assumptions + tool contracts + local references, not just portable instructions
Cost-benefit doesn't hold: 8 skills, 2-3 portable, building a general pipeline is over-engineered
Single source of truth claim is false — local is canonical, Munin is derived cache
documents/* is wrong namespace — passive artifacts vs mutable operational instructions have different trust and retrieval semantics
Security model incomplete: no namespace ACLs, no per-client write restrictions, write-only-from-laptop is operational assumption not enforced policy
Provenance and staleness have no detection mechanism — 'run /index-skills' is a reminder not a mitigation
Capability drift: agent/requires tags will rot without constant curation
Retrieval false positives: wrong operational prompt retrieval is worse than failed retrieval
Interaction-model mismatch: conversational confirmation loops don't transfer to autonomous agents
Sensitive workflow leakage: operational metadata (paths, account IDs, URL mappings) is sensitive beyond just credentials
Alternative A: store only curated portable playbooks, not sync all skills
Alternative C: extract reusable sub-artifacts (style profile) rather than whole skill containers
Security defense dodges operational consequences: still no trust model for who publishes, how consumers know entry is approved, what 'untrusted instruction' means in practice
Manual curation shifts canonical source ambiguity: if playbooks are independently edited in Munin, the relationship to local skills becomes editorial, not derivative
Revised proposal bundles dissimilar artifacts (playbooks, sub-artifacts, metadata) — these shouldn't be treated as one thing
A new namespace is not free — 2-3 artifacts don't justify the design overhead of conventions, retrieval rules, ownership rules
Demand is still assumed: no evidence other agents actually need or would benefit from these artifacts
